text: Today I'm craving lobster and stone crab, but I'm not about to go buy some when I can go catch my very own. So let me show you how we got these. Earlier when I went to the grocery store I saw that stone crab claws were selling between 30 and 50 dollars a pound and with my appetite there's no way I'm gonna be paying those prices. So I jumped in the water and started looking for my very own. It wasn't long before I came across the first stone crab of the day. This one's too small to eat but it's a good sign that they're around so I had to keep looking for a larger one. A little while later I came across this rock that caught my attention because it had a couple small lobsters on it and so when I looked underneath to see if there was a larger one hiding I ended up coming across this big stone crab so I carefully stuck my hand into one of the holes that way I could scare them out of the top and be able to have the first stone crab claw of the day. As soon as I get the stone crabs out of the hole I quickly try to grab the back legs that way they can't grab a hold of me with those giant claws because that would really hurt. In order to be able to harvest the bottom of the claw has to be longer than this gap so as you can see this one right here is plenty good. To remove the claw I poked the inside joint membrane and that causes the crab to release it all on its own which is actually their defense mechanism and then I just go let him go right back where I found him and he'll regrow his claw another day. Unlike most other types of crab where you keep the entire thing stone crabs you only keep the claws. Then I just kept repeating the same process of catching the crabs removing the large claws and letting them go so that they can regrow them another day and there were quite a few of them today that had some giant claws and while I was doing this I ended up being greeted by a very tiny little nurse shark. This is one of the smallest ones I've ever seen. After getting quite a few stone crab claws I ended up coming across this hole that had a couple small lobsters but after looking in the back of it I ended up finding the large one that was hiding behind them all so I scared it out of the hole and then used my lobster snare to go around its tail and pull it tightly closed and now we also have some lobster for dinner and then I just repeated the same process to catch one more. Back at the house I boiled the stone crab claws and then tossed them into an ice bath then I split the lobster tails in half coated them with some of my favorite seasonings and cooked them outside on the grill and just like that a little while later we have a homemade seafood platter. First up I'm going to be trying the lobster. Mmm, grilled lobster tails are pretty hard to beat but now it's time to try the stone crab claws. These are some of the biggest claws I think I've ever gotten so time to see how they taste. Mmm, I think stone crabs and mustard sauce has to be my all-time favorite. I could eat these every single day. Mmm, now I'm going to enjoy the rest of the platter and until next time get outside and see what you can find.
